scenario: A FEEL expression must compute duration in years and months between two dates: birthDate = date("1990-06-15") and currentDate = date("2026-05-14") → 35 years 10 months.
question: Which FEEL built-in fits "years-and-months duration"?

options.text: (date2 - date1) -- subtracting two FEEL date values yields a days and time duration type, not a years and months duration. The result expresses the difference in days and time units. Converting days to years and months requires additional calculation because months have different lengths. For calculating the exact number of complete years and months between two dates in FEEL, the correct function is years and months duration(date1, date2) which returns a years and months duration type that correctly accounts for calendar months and leap years.

duration(date2 - date1) -- wrapping date subtraction in the duration() function does not change the type of result. Date subtraction in FEEL always yields a days and time duration regardless of any wrapping. The duration() function parses ISO 8601 duration strings; it does not convert between duration types. To obtain a years and months duration from two date values, use the dedicated years and months duration(date1, date2) function which correctly computes the calendar difference in complete years and months, accounting for variable month lengths and leap years.

md:329-342 -- FEEL conversion built-in. Signature: years and months duration(from: date, to: date): years and months duration. Example from docs: years and months duration(date("2011-12-22"), date("2013-08-24")) → P1Y8M. Returns a value of type years and months duration (distinct from days and time duration). Useful for age calculations, contract terms, etc. Note: lives in the conversion category (not temporal), alongside duration(from) parsing and to json/from json.

dateDiff(date1, date2, "years") -- this function name and signature are invented and do not exist in the FEEL built-in function library. There is no dateDiff function in the FEEL specification. The correct FEEL function for computing the calendar difference between two dates in years and months is years and months duration(date1, date2). This function returns a years and months duration value like P2Y3M meaning 2 years and 3 months. The function accounts for calendar month lengths and leap years when computing the duration between the two date values.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL distinguishes two duration types: days and time duration (PT...H...M...S, P...D): for sub-day precision and exact wall-clock durations. years and months duration (P...Y...M): for calendar-based durations like age, contract terms -- where months aren't a fixed number of days. years and months duration(date1, date2) returns the year-month duration between two dates. For age, accessor functions like years and months on the duration value extract the components.

b.text: Incorrect. Date subtraction yields days and time duration.
